scope.summary: Implement IK-mode avatar pose editing that accepts end-effector joint targets, drives a chain IK solver (FABRIK or analytical 2-bone) over the tailor_avatars skeleton, and updates the linked ClothBodyProxy capsule positions for the next sim run
acceptance_criteria: A PoseEditRequest type (joint_name: String, target_position_mm: [f32;3]) is defined in tailor-solver/src/body/ik_pose.rs
The IK solver reaches the target end-effector within 5 mm in <= 20 iterations for a 2-bone chain (hip -> knee -> ankle)
The resulting joint transform chain is propagated to update all CollisionCapsule p0_mm/p1_mm values in the linked BPX- row
TailorBodyProxyUpdated is emitted after each successful pose edit commit
Existing garment sim runs on the same AVT- are not invalidated (prior sim results are historical; new runs pick up the updated proxy)
cargo test tailor_ik::test_two_bone_chain_reach and tailor_ik::test_ik_proxy_capsule_update both pass
scope.risk_if_missed: Operators and models cannot pose avatars without re-importing a full custom mesh. Pose variation for draping (seated, arms raised, A-pose vs T-pose) requires external tooling, breaking the self-contained workflow MD users expect.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
